# UC-17.1.26 · Cloudflare Zone HTTP 5xx and Origin Error Rate

> **Criticality:** High &middot; **Difficulty:** Beginner &middot; **Pillar:** Observability &middot; **Type:** Availability, Security &middot; **Status:** Draft

*When visitors to our websites get error pages instead of the content they expected, we track exactly what went wrong — was our website down, was it too slow to respond, or was there a security certificate problem — so the right team can fix it quickly.*

---

## Description

Elevated 5xx responses at the edge or from the origin indicate failing pools, overloaded applications, or deliberate application-layer attacks. Ray ID–level aggregation supports fast hand-off between CDN and application owners.

## Value

SRE teams track Cloudflare 5xx error rates per zone, separating edge errors (origin unreachable, timeout, SSL failures) from application errors (500/502/503), and measuring consumption against monthly error budgets to trigger reliability investments before SLAs are breached.

## Implementation

Create a Logpush job for `http_requests` to Splunk HEC; set sourcetype to `cloudflare:json` if following Cloudflare’s Splunk integration guide, or the dataset-specific sourcetype from the app’s Details section. Include `EdgeResponseStatus`, `OriginResponseStatus`, `ClientRequestHost`, and `RayID` in field selection. Alert when 5xx count exceeds a rolling baseline per hostname.

## Detailed Implementation

### Prerequisites
- Cloudflare Logpush to Splunk via HTTP Event Collector (HEC) or an S3 bucket. Dataset: `http_requests`. Data in `index=cloudflare` with `sourcetype=cloudflare:logpush:http_requests`. Key fields: `EdgeResponseStatus`, `OriginResponseStatus`, `ClientRequestHost` (zone), `ClientRequestURI`, `OriginIP`, `EdgeColoCode`, `CacheCacheStatus`, `EdgeTimeToFirstByteMs`.
- 5xx errors at the edge: Cloudflare cannot serve the request (520=unknown error from origin, 521=origin is down, 522=connection timed out, 523=origin unreachable, 524=origin timeout, 525=SSL handshake failed, 526=invalid SSL certificate). 5xx from origin: the application itself is failing (500=internal error, 502=bad gateway, 503=service unavailable).
- Create a `cloudflare_zones.csv` lookup: `zone_host,zone_name,team_owner,error_budget_pct` mapping zones to ownership.

### Step 1 — Configure data collection
Set up Logpush: Cloudflare Dashboard > Analytics > Logs > Logpush > Create job > destination Splunk (HEC endpoint). Select the `http_requests` dataset and include all fields.

Verify:
```spl
index=cloudflare sourcetype="cloudflare:logpush:http_requests" earliest=-1h
| stats count by EdgeResponseStatus
```

### Step 2 — Create the search and alert

Primary search — 5xx error rate by zone:
```spl
index=cloudflare sourcetype="cloudflare:logpush:http_requests" earliest=-24h
| eval is_5xx=if(EdgeResponseStatus>=500, 1, 0), is_origin_5xx=if(OriginResponseStatus>=500, 1, 0)
| timechart span=5m sum(is_5xx) as edge_5xx sum(is_origin_5xx) as origin_5xx count as total by ClientRequestHost limit=10
```

#### Understanding this SPL: Separates edge 5xx (Cloudflare-to-origin problems) from origin 5xx (application problems). A spike in edge 5xx (especially 521/522/523) means the origin server is down or unreachable — this is an infrastructure issue. A spike in origin 5xx (500/502/503) means the application is failing — this is a development or capacity issue. Tracking by zone shows which property is affected.

Error-code breakdown:
```spl
index=cloudflare sourcetype="cloudflare:logpush:http_requests" EdgeResponseStatus>=500 earliest=-24h
| stats count by EdgeResponseStatus, OriginResponseStatus, ClientRequestHost
| lookup cloudflare_zones.csv zone_host as ClientRequestHost OUTPUT zone_name team_owner
| sort - count
```

Error budget tracking:
```spl
index=cloudflare sourcetype="cloudflare:logpush:http_requests" earliest=-30d
| stats count(eval(EdgeResponseStatus>=500)) as errors count as total by ClientRequestHost
| eval error_rate_pct=round(errors/total*100, 3)
| lookup cloudflare_zones.csv zone_host as ClientRequestHost OUTPUT error_budget_pct zone_name
| eval budget_remaining=round(tonumber(error_budget_pct) - error_rate_pct, 3)
| eval budget_status=if(budget_remaining > 0, "Within Budget", "EXCEEDED")
| sort budget_remaining
```

### Step 3 — Validate
(a) In Cloudflare Dashboard > Analytics > Traffic > filter by 5xx. Compare counts with Splunk.
(b) Send a request to a known-broken endpoint and verify the 5xx appears.

### Step 4 — Operationalize
Dashboard: Row 1 — Error rate trend by zone (timechart). Row 2 — Error code breakdown. Row 3 — Error budget remaining.
Alert: When 5xx error rate exceeds 1% of total requests in any 5-minute window.

Runbook:
1. **521/523 spike** — Origin server down. Check origin health and restart services.
2. **522/524 spike** — Origin timeout. Server may be overloaded. Scale up or investigate slow queries.
3. **525/526 spike** — SSL certificate issue on origin. Renew or reconfigure.
4. **500 from origin** — Application bug. Forward to development team with request URI and timestamp.

### Step 5 — Troubleshooting
- **No Logpush data** — Check Cloudflare Logpush job status. Ensure HEC token is valid and index is correct.
- **`OriginResponseStatus` always 0** — Request served from cache (no origin contact). Filter: `CacheCacheStatus!=hit`.

## SPL

```spl
index=web sourcetype="cloudflare:json" earliest=-1h
| eval edge_status=tonumber(EdgeResponseStatus)
| eval origin_status=tonumber(OriginResponseStatus)
| where edge_status>=500 OR origin_status>=500
| stats count by ClientRequestHost, EdgeResponseStatus, OriginResponseStatus, RayID
| sort -count
| head 50
```

## CIM SPL

```spl
| tstats `summariesonly` count
  from datamodel=Web.Web
  by Web.user, Web.url, Web.http_method span=1h
| sort - count
```

## Visualization

Time chart (5xx rate), Table (hostname, edge vs origin status), Single value (SLA breach indicator).

## Known False Positives

Anomalies from new SaaS app rollouts, browser updates, or vendor URL category changes.

## References

- [Cloudflare Docs — Enable Logpush to Splunk](https://developers.cloudflare.com/logs/logpush/logpush-job/enable-destinations/splunk/)
- [Splunkbase — Cloudflare App for Splunk](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/4501)
